title: 怪屋
summary:
After a car crash at Fletchers Quarry results in the deaths of two passengers, Margarita Leonides mourns her child, Josephine. The narrator discovers concealed letters written by Edith de Havilland behind a copper teapot in an oak cabinet. One letter, addressed to Inspector Taverna, is a confession in which Edith takes full responsibility for the murders of Aristide Leonidis and the nursemaid Janet Lovell to protect Brenda Leonidis and Lawrence Brown. Edith notes that Dr. Mitchell Chaworth can confirm she has only months to live.

The narrator finds a second, thicker letter addressed to Charles, which contains Josephine's small black notebook. Upon reading the notebook, the narrator and Sophia realize that Josephine was the true killer. The notebook contains entries written in a child's handwriting, including the statement, "I killed my grandfather today." Josephine's motives were rooted in her desire to study ballet and her resentment toward family members. She had orchestrated a complex scheme, including a staged attack on herself involving a door stopper and a laundry room chair, to divert suspicion. She also poisoned the nursemaid using digitalis stolen from Edith's room to vent her anger.

The narrator reflects on Josephine's abnormal, self-centered nature, which he attributes to her family's genetic traits. Edith had discovered the notebook in an abandoned doghouse and acted out of a misguided sense of love to protect the child from the consequences of her crimes. Following the revelation, Charles proposes marriage to Sophia, promising to take her to Persia to start a new life. Charles eventually informs his father that Josephine, not Edith, was the killer, a conclusion his father admits he had already reached.
